was the 17th edition of the annual international club football competition held in the [AFC](/wiki/Asian_Football_Confederation "Asian Football Confederation") region (Asia). It determined that year's club champion of [association football](/wiki/Football_(soccer) "Football (soccer)") in [Asia](/wiki/Asia "Asia").

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

17th edition of premier club football tournament organized by the AFC

1997–1998 Asian Club Championship

| Runners-up | [China](/wiki/China "China") [Dalian Wanda](/wiki/Dalian_Shide "Dalian Shide") |

| ← [1996–97](/wiki/1996%E2%80%9397_Asian_Club_Championship "1996–97 Asian Club Championship")  [1998–99](/wiki/1998%E2%80%9399_Asian_Club_Championship "1998–99 Asian Club Championship") → | |

International football competition

The **1997–98 [Asian Club Championship](/wiki/Asian_Club_Championship "Asian Club Championship")** was the 17th edition of the annual international club football competition held in the [AFC](/wiki/Asian_Football_Confederation "Asian Football Confederation") region (Asia). It determined that year's club champion of [association football](/wiki/Football_(soccer) "Football (soccer)") in [Asia](/wiki/Asia "Asia").

[Pohang Steelers](/wiki/Pohang_Steelers "Pohang Steelers") of [South Korea](/wiki/South_Korea "South Korea") won their 2nd consecutive Asian Champions title, beating [Dalian Wanda](/wiki/Dalian_Wanda "Dalian Wanda") 6–5 on penalties.
